thread.title: President-elect Trump endorses Randy Fine to run for congressional seat in Daytona area
thread.title_full: President-elect Trump endorses Randy Fine to run for congressional seat in Daytona area

author: , Florida Today
title: President-elect Trump endorses Randy Fine to run for congressional seat in Daytona area
text:
President-elect Trump endorses Randy Fine to run for congressional seat in Daytona area Florida Today President-elect Trump on Saturday endorsed Brevard's Randy Fine to seek the 6th Congressional District seat opened by the nomination of Matt Waltz to be the next national security adviser .
"I am hearing that America First Patriot Randy Fine is considering launching his Campaign for Congress in Florida's 6th Congressional District!" Trump posted on the Truth Social platform. "Should he decide to enter this Race, Randy Fine has my Complete and Total Endorsement. RUN, RANDY, RUN!"
Fine, who just won a Florida Senate seat representing Brevard County, has not announced a plan to run for Congress. But he responded to Trump's endorsement by indicating that an announcement would come soon. "Your confidence is overwhelming and I will have news to share soon!" Fine wrote on X, over a copy of Trump's post.
Clearly Mom wasted no time once she made it upstairs.
Mr. President, G-d saved you on that day in Butler so that you could save the world. It would be the honor of my life to be one of your footsoldiers. Your confidence is overwhelming and I will have news to share soon! pic.twitter.com/Zt9Tj3rF6e
— Senator Randy Fine (@VoteRandyFine) November 23, 2024 Fine did not immediately respond to a request to comment.
Where is the 6th Congressional District? The 6th Congressional District represents the Daytona Beach area. Voters in the 6th Congressional District − made up of northern Volusia County, all of Flagler and Putnam counties, plus parts of St. Johns, Marion and Lake counties − just reelected Waltz to a fourth term. But Trump tapped Waltz to be his national security adviser .
Waltz's exit from Congress will require Gov. Ron DeSantis to announce a special election to fill the vacancy, and that's what Trump is endorsing Fine for.
The U.S. Constitution doesn't require candidates to be a resident of the congressional district they are seeking as long as they reside in the state . However, many do ultimately establish residency in a district they seek to represent. Fine currently lives in southern Brevard County.
Fine threw his support to Trump in presidential primary Fine has served in the Florida House of Representatives since 2016 and was prevented from running again because of term limits. He sought the Florida Senate District 19 seat, which he easily won. Senate District 19 covers most of Central and South Brevard.
Fine had become the first high-profile GOP state legislator to publicly turn on DeSantis last year.
Fine, previously among the governor's strongest allies in the Florida House, made national headlines in October 2023 when he broke with DeSantis and changed his endorsement in the GOP presidential primary to Trump, calling out what he said was DeSantis' inaction in the face of rising antisemitism across the state.
The move was widely seen as the first major crack in the governor's iron-clad support from the Republican-controlled Florida Legislature in his ultimately doomed run for president. Trump later endorsed Fine in his state Senate run.
Fine missed the Legislature's post-election Organization Session on Nov. 19, in which the chambers elected their new leaders, because of his mother's death. After Trump's endorsement, Fine noted, "Clearly Mom wasted no time once she made it upstairs."
In response to Trump's endorsement saying he would be "an INCREDIBLE Fighter," Fine said it "would be the honor of my life to be one of your footsoldiers."
(This story was updated to add new information.)
